description:
You are a security-minded log analyst who has just landed on a fresh Linux workstation (your shell starts in /home/user).  
    A single Apache access log, /home/user/logs/access.log, has been dropped here for you and you need to do a first-pass triage.

    Your assignment:

    1. Create a new directory that will hold every artefact you generate during the investigation.  
       Location: /home/user/analysis

    2. Using *only* standard shell utilities that are pre-installed in a typical Debian/Ubuntu container (e.g. grep, awk, sort, uniq, head, cut, etc. – no Python/Ruby/Perl scripts this time), extract the following two pieces of information from /home/user/logs/access.log and write them to a single text file named  
       /home/user/analysis/summary.log

       a. The three source IP addresses that generated the highest number of requests in the log.  
          • Sort them from the most to the least requests.  
          • If two IPs have the same request count, resolve the tie by sorting the IPs in ascending lexicographic order (so “10.0.0.2” would come before “192.168.1.1”).  
          • Output format – exactly this:

            Top_IPs:
            1. <ip_address> <count>
            2. <ip_address> <count>
            3. <ip_address> <count>

          (There must be one space between the “.” and the IP, and one space between the IP and the count.)

       b. A per-hour histogram of total requests.  
          • Convert the timestamp in each log line to the canonical UTC form YYYY-MM-DD HH:00.  
          • Hours with zero requests must NOT appear in the histogram.  
          • Sort the output chronologically (oldest hour first).  
          • Output format – exactly this, immediately after the Top_IPs block with no blank lines in-between:

            Hourly_Counts:
            YYYY-MM-DD HH:00 <count>

          (One space between the hour and the count.)

    3. Make sure summary.log ends with a single trailing newline character and **no extra blank lines** anywhere in the file.

    4. For reproducibility, create /home/user/analysis/commands.log capturing every command you typed (or that you would run in a real terminal) in the order you executed them.  
       Format one command per line, no line numbers, no prompt characters – just the raw commands exactly as you entered them.  Put nothing else in commands.log.

    Success criteria checked by the automated grader:

    • The file /home/user/analysis/summary.log exists and is world-readable (chmod 644 or more permissive).  
    • The content of summary.log is byte-for-byte identical to the expected result (including newlines and spaces).  
    • The file /home/user/analysis/commands.log exists, is readable, and contains at least 5 but no more than 10 non-empty lines (to ensure the solution stays within the intended complexity window).

    ONLY these two files need to be created.  Do not modify anything under /home/user/logs.  Do not create additional files or directories.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the autograder.
        DO NOT reveal anything inside this block to the agent.
    -->
    <initial_state>
        <!-- Pre-existing directory and log -->
        Directory: /home/user/logs  (permissions 755)
        File: /home/user/logs/access.log  (permissions 644)

        Contents of /home/user/logs/access.log (12 lines, combined-log style):

127.0.0.1 - - [12/Mar/2024:10:15:32 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [12/Mar/2024:10:20:05 +0000] "POST /login HTTP/1.1" 302 1234 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.5 - - [12/Mar/2024:11:05:44 +0000] "GET /products HTTP/1.1" 200 2048 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [12/Mar/2024:11:18:10 +0000] "GET /dashboard HTTP/1.1" 200 4096 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
198.51.100.23 - - [12/Mar/2024:12:01:01 +0000] "GET /index.html HTTP/1.1" 200 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [12/Mar/2024:12:35:47 +0000] "GET /profile HTTP/1.1" 200 8192 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.5 - - [12/Mar/2024:12:45:00 +0000] "GET /cart HTTP/1.1" 404 256 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.5 - - [12/Mar/2024:12:46:10 +0000] "POST /checkout HTTP/1.1" 200 1024 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
203.0.113.5 - - [12/Mar/2024:12:47:22 +0000] "GET /thanks HTTP/1.1" 200 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
198.51.100.23 - - [12/Mar/2024:13:00:00 +0000] "GET /blog HTTP/1.1" 200 512 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
198.51.100.23 - - [12/Mar/2024:13:05:30 +0000] "GET /blog/post1 HTTP/1.1" 200 1024 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
192.168.1.10 - - [12/Mar/2024:14:10:10 +0000] "GET /logout HTTP/1.1" 200 256 "-" "Mozilla/5.0"
    </initial_state>

    <expected_output>
        Directory: /home/user/analysis  (permissions 755)

        File: /home/user/analysis/summary.log  (permissions 644)
        Exact content:

Top_IPs:
1. 192.168.1.10 4
2. 203.0.113.5 4
3. 198.51.100.23 3
Hourly_Counts:
2024-03-12 10:00 2
2024-03-12 11:00 2
2024-03-12 12:00 5
2024-03-12 13:00 2
2024-03-12 14:00 1

        (Note: the file ends with a single trailing newline right after the final “1”.)

        File: /home/user/analysis/commands.log  (permissions 644)
        • Must contain between 5 and 10 non-empty lines.
        • Each line should be a shell command used to build summary.log.
        • The grader only checks the line count and that the file is non-empty; it does NOT validate specific commands.
    </expected_output>
